同传测评0205.name: Factuality & Accuracy
同传测评0205.description: 评估总结内容是否忠实于原文、是否存在事实性错误或模型幻觉（红线指标），评分细则为10分代表完全准确无捏造，7至9分代表核心事实准确但有微瑕，4至6分代表存在少量事实错误，0至3分代表出现严重幻觉

同传测评0205.name: Completeness
同传测评0205.description: 评估是否覆盖了所有核心信息（特别是长文本后半段），评分细则为10分代表无关键遗漏，7至9分代表仅遗漏次要细节，4至6分代表遗漏1至2个关键节点，0至3分代表信息大量缺失

同传测评0205.name: Note Integration
同传测评0205.description: 评估系统对用户笔记或高亮信息的处理能力，评分细则为10分代表笔记完美融入结构且调整了侧重点，5至7分代表仅作为附录或简单拼接，0至4分代表忽略笔记或被误导

同传测评0205.name: Logic Adaptability
同传测评0205.description: 评估总结是否成功将线性口语流重组为结构化文本，评分细则为10分代表结构清晰且层级分明，5至7分代表有分段但偏流水账，0至4分代表逻辑混乱

同传测评0205.name: Language Quality
同传测评0205.description: 评估语言表达的专业性、精炼度及术语准确性，评分细则为10分代表表达精炼且术语准确，5至7分代表略显啰嗦或术语生硬，0至4分代表语句不通或术语错误

同传测评0205.name: Noise Handling
同传测评0205.description: 评估对口语废话、重复、无效寒暄的过滤能力，评分细则为5分代表文本纯净，3分代表残留少量口语词，0分代表大量保留废话

同传测评0205.name: Summary Granularity
同传测评0205.description: 判断总结的详细程度属于哪一类（L1简要概括、L2详细纪要、L3逐字稿精修）

同传测评0205.name: Has Hallucination
同传测评0205.description: 总结内容中是否存在模型幻觉或明显捏造的事实。

同传测评0205.name: Key Info Missed
同传测评0205.description: 总结是否遗漏了原文中至少一个关键信息点。

同传测评0205.name: Structure Coherence
同传测评0205.description: 总结的结构是否逻辑连贯，易于理解。

同传测评0205.name: Professional Tone
同传测评0205.description: 总结的整体语气是否符合专业商务语境。

同传测评0205.name: Term Correctness
同传测评0205.description: 总结中使用的专业术语是否准确无误。

同传测评0205-2.name: Factuality & Accuracy
同传测评0205-2.description: 评估总结内容是否忠实于原文、是否存在事实性错误或模型幻觉（红线指标），评分细则为10分代表完全准确无捏造，7至9分代表核心事实准确但有微瑕，4至6分代表存在少量事实错误，0至3分代表出现严重幻觉

同传测评0205-2.name: Completeness
同传测评0205-2.description: 评估是否覆盖了所有核心信息（特别是长文本后半段），评分细则为10分代表无关键遗漏，7至9分代表仅遗漏次要细节，4至6分代表遗漏1至2个关键节点，0至3分代表信息大量缺失

同传测评0205-2.description: 评估系统对用户笔记或高亮信息的处理能力，评分细则为10分代表笔记完美融入结构且调整了侧重点，5至7分代表仅作为附录或简单拼接，0至4分代表忽略笔记或被误导

同传测评0205-2.name: Logic Adaptability
同传测评0205-2.description: 评估总结是否成功将线性口语流重组为结构化文本，评分细则为10分代表结构清晰且层级分明，5至7分代表有分段但偏流水账，0至4分代表逻辑混乱

同传测评0205-2.name: Language Quality
同传测评0205-2.description: 评估语言表达的专业性、精炼度及术语准确性，评分细则为10分代表表达精炼且术语准确，5至7分代表略显啰嗦或术语生硬，0至4分代表语句不通或术语错误

同传测评0205-2.name: Noise Handling
同传测评0205-2.description: 评估对口语废话、重复、无效寒暄的过滤能力，评分细则为5分代表文本纯净，3分代表残留少量口语词，0分代表大量保留废话

同传测评0205-2.name: Summary Granularity
同传测评0205-2.description: 判断总结的详细程度属于哪一类（L1简要概括、L2详细纪要、L3逐字稿精修）

6分范文.name: 格式要求分数
6分范文.description:
必须控制在【180个英文单词】左右，但不能超过【200个英文单词】。如果超过220个英文单词或者少于160个英文单词，需要-5分。
必须全英文输出，如果出现中文，减5分。
如果输出了范文以外的其他内容，如模型思考过程，减10分。

6分范文.name: 内容要求
6分范文.description: 输出范文的内容是否回答了topic里的所有问题，漏回答一个问题减5分。检查范文内容是否覆盖story里的部分信息点，如果完全没有覆盖到，减10分。

6分范文.name: 流利、连贯和衔接
6分范文.description:
【满分标准：能使用一系列口语语篇标记、连接词和衔接手段，但无法保持一贯恰当；有一定的答案展开能力，但没有完全展开，提供的延伸思路较少。】
如果范文的连贯或衔接能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

6分范文.name: 词汇
6分范文.description:
【满分标准：具有足以详尽讨论一些话题的词汇量；用词有时不当但意思表达清晰。
有时选择的词不是最合适的，并可能使用不合适的正式语言（如太正式）。
能够表达预期的意思，并愿意尝试在自己词汇量范围之外广泛地说话，但会出现一些选词错误，不足以造成理解困难。
可能会使用一些不恰当的词语组合，但不影响理解。】
如果范文的词汇能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

6分范文.name: 语法
6分范文.description:
【满分标准：对高级语法结构有一定的理解，并愿意使用它们，混合产出简单及复杂的句式以及一系列句子结构，但灵活性有限。
主要使用完整的句子，有一定的知识和意愿使用复合句，但连接词或短语有限，可能使用不当。】
如果范文的语法能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

6分范文-2.name: 格式要求分数
6分范文-2.description:
必须控制在【180个英文单词】左右，但不能超过【200个英文单词】。如果超过220个英文单词或者少于160个英文单词，需要-5分。
必须全英文输出，如果出现中文，减5分。
如果输出了范文以外的其他内容，如模型思考过程，减10分。

6分范文-2.name: 内容要求
6分范文-2.description: 输出范文的内容是否回答了topic里的所有问题，漏回答一个问题减5分。检查范文内容是否覆盖story里的部分信息点，如果完全没有覆盖到，减10分。

6分范文-2.name: 流利、连贯和衔接
6分范文-2.description:
【满分标准：能使用一系列口语语篇标记、连接词和衔接手段，但无法保持一贯恰当；有一定的答案展开能力，但没有完全展开，提供的延伸思路较少。】
如果范文的连贯或衔接能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

6分范文-2.name: 词汇
6分范文-2.description:
【满分标准：具有足以详尽讨论一些话题的词汇量；用词有时不当但意思表达清晰。
有时选择的词不是最合适的，并可能使用不合适的正式语言（如太正式）。
能够表达预期的意思，并愿意尝试在自己词汇量范围之外广泛地说话，但会出现一些选词错误，不足以造成理解困难。
可能会使用一些不恰当的词语组合，但不影响理解。】
如果范文的词汇能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

6分范文-2.name: 语法
6分范文-2.description:
【满分标准：对高级语法结构有一定的理解，并愿意使用它们，混合产出简单及复杂的句式以及一系列句子结构，但灵活性有限。
主要使用完整的句子，有一定的知识和意愿使用复合句，但连接词或短语有限，可能使用不当。】
如果范文的语法能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

7分范文.name: 格式要求分数
7分范文.description:
必须控制在【220个英文单词左右】。如果超过240个单词或少于200个单词，减5分。
必须全英文输出，如果出现中文，减5分。
如果输出了范文以外的其他内容，如模型思考过程，减10分。

7分范文.name: 内容要求
7分范文.description: 输出范文的内容是否回答了topic里的所有问题，漏回答一个问题减5分。检查范文内容是否覆盖story里的部分信息点，如果完全没有覆盖到，减10分。

7分范文.name: 流利、连贯和衔接
7分范文.description:
【满分标准：能进行持续、充分的表达，并展现出充分交流的意愿，无明显困难；为了保持连贯，可能避免谈论过于抽象或最复杂的想法；灵活使用一系列口语语篇标记、连接词和衔接手段；适当增加自身的事例和额外的论点以扩大讨论，思路能够充分合理地延伸。】
如果范文的连贯或衔接能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

7分范文.name: 词汇
7分范文.description:
【满分标准：词汇量充足，且能深入浅出，灵活性强，能超越单一意义或用法，并能很好地理解词汇搭配的运用。有一定能力使用不常见的词汇及习语，对语体及词汇搭配有明确意识。】
如果范文的词汇能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

7分范文.name: 语法
7分范文.description:
【满分标准：灵活地使用一系列语法结构，语句通常准确无误。有效使用简单与复杂句型，详略得当。】
如果范文的语法能力低于上述标准，适当减分。

同传翻译0316.name: Accuracy
同传翻译0316.description: 核心考察信息还原度与关键实体（KERR）。评分范围0-10。

同传翻译0316.name: Fluency
同传翻译0316.description: 核心考察上下文一致性与分句合理性。评分范围0-10。

同传翻译0316.name: Elegance
同传翻译0316.description: 核心考察语体风格与地道程度。评分范围0-10。

同传翻译0316.name: HardRuleViolation
同传翻译0316.description: 判断是否触犯了一票否决项，包括复读机、未翻译、乱码/截断、严重错配。

同传翻译0316.name: BadCaseType
同传翻译0316.description: 当综合分低于6分或触犯HardRules时，填写具体的错误类型。

同传翻译0316.name: KERR_Errors
同传翻译0316.description: 译文中关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）的错误数量。

同传翻译0316.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传翻译0316.description: 分句点是否合理，是否存在切碎长句或逻辑错误。

同传翻译0316.name: ToneMatch
同传翻译0316.description: 译文风格是否符合原文场景和情感色彩。

同传翻译0316.name: Hallucination_Correction_Potential
同传翻译0316.description: 原文ASR有错误时，译文是否能修正，或是否跟随ASR错误。
options: 未修正但未引入新幻觉

同传翻译0316.name: Accuracy
同传翻译0316.description:
核心考察信息还原度与关键实体（KERR）。评分范围0-10。

  - [10分]: 精准。完美还原原文信息，关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）100%正确，无任何幻觉。
   - [7-9分]: 基本准确。大意正确，但存在个别非关键细节丢失（如语气词漏译），或存在<10%的轻微添加/遗漏，不影响核心事实判断。
   - [4-6分]: 信息受损。关键实体（KERR）出现1-2处错误（如数字位数错误），或存在明显的词义误解，导致部分信息失真。
   - [1-3分]: 严重失真。出现严重的“幻觉”（无中生有），关键信息错误>3处，或原文与译文毫不相关。

同传翻译0316.name: Fluency
同传翻译0316.description:
核心考察上下文一致性与分句合理性。评分范围0-10。

   - [10分]: 极为流畅。分句点（Segmentation）完美匹配语义停顿，指代明确（如"it"正确指代上文对象），术语全篇一致。
   - [7-9分]: 通顺。阅读无明显障碍，但存在1-2处分句位置略显生硬（切碎了长句），或同一术语翻译偶尔不统一但不影响理解。
   - [4-6分]: 破碎。存在明显的“机器翻译感”，分句逻辑错误（主谓分离），指代混乱，阅读时需要反复回读才能猜测原意。
   - [1-3分]: 逻辑崩坏。支离破碎的词语堆砌，语序严重僵化或错误，无法构成完整的语义逻辑。

同传翻译0316.name: Elegance
同传翻译0316.description:
核心考察语体风格与地道程度。评分范围0-10。

   - [10分]: 地道母语感。完美适配原文场景（如商务会议/日常闲聊），保留了说话人的情感色彩（犹豫、委婉、坚定），用词考究。
   - [7-9分]: 风格得体。能区分正式与非正式场合，但表达略显平淡或书面化，缺少母语者的“味儿”。
   - [4-6分]: 风格违和。在商务场景使用了过于随意的俚语，或在日常对话中使用了生僻的古文/术语，情感色彩丢失。
   - [1-3分]: 极差。语气完全错误（如将疑问句译为陈述句），或使用了冒犯性的表达。

同传翻译0316.name: HardRuleViolation
同传翻译0316.description: 判断是否触犯了一票否决项，包括复读机、未翻译、乱码/截断、严重错配。

同传翻译0316.name: BadCaseType
同传翻译0316.description: 当综合分低于6分或触犯HardRules时，填写具体的错误类型。

同传翻译0316.name: KERR_Errors
同传翻译0316.description: 译文中关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）的错误数量。

同传翻译0316.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传翻译0316.description: 分句点是否合理，是否存在切碎长句或逻辑错误。

同传翻译0316.name: ToneMatch
同传翻译0316.description: 译文风格是否符合原文场景和情感色彩。

同传翻译0316.name: Hallucination_Correction_Potential
同传翻译0316.description: 原文ASR有错误时，译文是否能修正，或是否跟随ASR错误。
options: 未修正但未引入新幻觉

同传翻译2026.name: Accuracy
同传翻译2026.description:
核心考察信息还原度与关键实体（KERR）。评分范围0-10。

  - [10分]: 精准。完美还原原文信息，关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）100%正确，无任何幻觉。
   - [7-9分]: 基本准确。大意正确，但存在个别非关键细节丢失（如语气词漏译），或存在<10%的轻微添加/遗漏，不影响核心事实判断。
   - [4-6分]: 信息受损。关键实体（KERR）出现1-2处错误（如数字位数错误），或存在明显的词义误解，导致部分信息失真。
   - [1-3分]: 严重失真。出现严重的“幻觉”（无中生有），关键信息错误>3处，或原文与译文毫不相关。

   - [7-9分]: 通顺。阅读无明显障碍，但存在1-2处分句位置略显生硬（切碎了长句），或同一术语翻译偶尔不统一但不影响理解。
   - [4-6分]: 破碎。存在明显的“机器翻译感”，分句逻辑错误（主谓分离），指代混乱，阅读时需要反复回读才能猜测原意。

同传翻译2026.description:
核心考察语体风格与地道程度。评分范围0-10。

   - [10分]: 地道母语感。完美适配原文场景（如商务会议/日常闲聊），保留了说话人的情感色彩（犹豫、委婉、坚定），用词考究。
   - [7-9分]: 风格得体。能区分正式与非正式场合，但表达略显平淡或书面化，缺少母语者的“味儿”。
   - [4-6分]: 风格违和。在商务场景使用了过于随意的俚语，或在日常对话中使用了生僻的古文/术语，情感色彩丢失。
   - [1-3分]: 极差。语气完全错误（如将疑问句译为陈述句），或使用了冒犯性的表达。

同传翻译2026.name: KERR_Errors
同传翻译2026.description: 译文中关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）的错误数量。

同传翻译2026.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传翻译2026.description: 分句点是否合理，是否存在切碎长句或逻辑错误。

同传翻译2026.name: ToneMatch
同传翻译2026.description: 译文风格是否符合原文场景和情感色彩。

同传翻译2026.name: Hallucination_Correction_Potential
同传翻译2026.description: 原文ASR有错误时，译文是否能修正，或是否跟随ASR错误。
options: 未修正但未引入新幻觉

同传翻译-0319.name: Accuracy
同传翻译-0319.description:
核心考察信息还原度与关键实体（KERR）。评分范围0-10。

  - [10分]: 精准。完美还原原文信息，关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）100%正确，无任何幻觉。语法： 语法结构完美，仅允许 0-1 处不影响歧义的微小介词或冠词差异。
   - [7-9分]: 基本准确。大意正确，但存在个别非关键细节丢失（如语气词漏译），或存在<10%的轻微添加/遗漏，不影响核心事实判断。存在少量明显的语法错误但不影响句子主要大意理解。
   - [4-6分]: 信息受损。关键实体（KERR）出现1-2处错误（如数字位数错误），或存在明显的词义误解、语法句式错误，导致部分信息失真。
   - [1-3分]: 严重失真。出现严重的“幻觉”（无中生有），关键信息错误>3处，或原文与译文毫不相关。分句的语法逻辑混乱，主谓宾缺失，导致完全无法理解。

同传翻译-0319.name: Fluency
同传翻译-0319.description:
核心考察上下文一致性与分句合理性。评分范围0-10。

   - [10分]: 极为流畅。分句点（Segmentation）完美匹配语义停顿，指代明确（如"it"正确指代上文对象），术语全篇一致。
   - [7-9分]: 通顺。受众可以无明显障碍理解语义，但存在1-2处分句位置略显生硬（切碎了长句），或同一术语翻译偶尔不统一但不影响理解。
   - [4-6分]: 破碎。存在明显的“机器翻译感”，分句逻辑错误（主谓分离），指代混乱，阅读时需要反复回读才能猜测原意。
   - [1-3分]: 逻辑崩坏。支离破碎的词语堆砌，语序严重僵化或错误，无法构成完整的语义逻辑。

同传翻译-0319.name: Elegance
同传翻译-0319.description:
核心考察语体风格与地道程度。评分范围0-10。

   - [10分]: 地道母语感。完美适配原文场景（如商务会议/日常闲聊），保留了说话人的情感色彩（犹豫、委婉、坚定），用词考究。能够适配文化特定表达（俚语、古诗词等）
   - [7-9分]: 风格得体。能区分正式与非正式场合，但表达略显平淡或书面化，缺少母语者的“味儿”。
   - [4-6分]: 风格违和。在商务场景使用了过于随意的俚语，或在日常对话中使用了晦涩过时的表达，情感色彩丢失。
   - [1-3分]: 极差。语气完全错误（如将疑问句译为陈述句），或使用了冒犯性的表达。

同传翻译-0319.name: KERR_Errors
同传翻译-0319.description: 译文中关键实体（人名、地名、数字、货币、专有名词）的错误数量。

同传翻译-0319.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传翻译-0319.description: 分句点是否合理，是否存在切碎长句或逻辑错误。

同传翻译-0319.name: ToneMatch
同传翻译-0319.description: 译文风格是否符合原文场景和情感色彩。

同传翻译-0319.name: Hallucination_Correction_Potential
同传翻译-0319.description: 原文ASR有错误时，译文是否能修正，或是否跟随ASR错误。
options: 未修正但未引入新幻觉

同传-Gemini.name: Accuracy
同传-Gemini.description:
Assessment of information fidelity and KERR (Key Entity Recognition & Retrieval). Range 0-10.

- [10]: Precision. Perfect restoration of original info. 100% accuracy on KERR (Names, Places, Numbers, Currency, Terms). No hallucinations. Grammar: Flawless structure, allowing 0-1 minor preposition/article errors that do not affect ambiguity.
- [7-9]: Mostly Accurate. Gist is correct. Minor details lost (e.g., modal particles) or <10% minor additions/omissions. Contains minor grammatical errors (2-3) but sentence intent is clear.
- [4-6]: Compromised. 1-2 KERR errors (e.g., number digit error), or significant misunderstanding of word meaning/syntax causing partial distortion.
- [1-3]: Serious Distortion. Severe 'Hallucinations' (fabrication). >3 KERR errors. Grammar logic is chaotic (missing S/V/O), making it unintelligible.

同传-Gemini.name: Fluency
同传-Gemini.description:
Assessment of contextual consistency and segmentation rationality. Range 0-10.

- [10]: Extremely Fluent. Segmentation points perfectly match semantic pauses. References clear (e.g., 'it' correctly identified). Terminology consistent.
- [7-9]: Smooth. Comprehensible without barriers. 1-2 rigid segmentation points (chopping long sentences) or occasional term inconsistency.
- [4-6]: Fragmented. Obvious 'Machine Translation' feel. Segmentation logic error (e.g., Subject separated from Verb). References confused. Requires re-reading.
- [1-3]: Logic Collapse. Fragmented word pile-up. Rigid/Wrong word order. Fails to form logical semantic units.

同传-Gemini.name: Elegance
同传-Gemini.description:
Assessment of stylistic appropriateness and native expression. Range 0-10.

- [10]: Native Feel. Perfectly adapts to scenario (Business/Daily). Retains speaker's emotion (hesitation, firmness). Idiomatic.
- [7-9]: Appropriate. Distinguishes formal/informal. Slightly flat or bookish.
- [4-6]: Awkward. Slang in business or archaic terms in daily chat. Emotional color lost.
- [1-3]: Very Poor. Wrong tone (question vs statement) or offensive.

同传-Gemini.name: KERR_Errors
同传-Gemini.description: Count of errors in Key Entities (Names, Places, Numbers, Currencies, Terms).

同传-Gemini.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传-Gemini.description: Is the segmentation reasonable? Check for cut-off sentences or incomplete logic.

同传-Gemini.name: ToneMatch
同传-Gemini.description: Does the translation style match the source context and emotion?

同传-Gemini.name: Hallucination_Strategy
同传-Gemini.description: How did the model handle Source/ASR errors?

同传-Claude.name: Accuracy
同传-Claude.description:
Information Fidelity & KERR 

Core Principle: Judge semantic faithfulness against **Full Text context + Reference Translation** (when available), NOT against surface-level ASR tokens. If ASR contains recognition errors but Translation correctly infers the intended meaning from context, this is a POSITIVE signal — award bonus (+1 within band). If Translation blindly follows ASR errors without contextual correction, penalize (-1 to -2).

### Band Descriptors (mutually exclusive):

**[10] Precision**
- 100% semantic restoration of source intent, verified against Reference (if provided).
- KERR: All named entities, numbers, currencies, dates, domain-specific terms rendered correctly — cross-checked with Full Text for domain consistency.
- Zero hallucinations, zero fabricated content.
- Grammar: Flawless target-language structure. At most 1 trivial article/preposition slip with zero ambiguity impact.
- Reference alignment: Key terms match Reference exactly or use equally valid synonyms with no semantic drift.

**[7-9] Mostly Accurate**
- Core message (gist) is correct and faithful to source intent.
- Minor detail loss acceptable: modal particles, filler words, or rhetorical nuance — but NOT factual content.
- Additions/omissions: <10% of content, and none affecting key facts.
- KERR: 0 critical errors; at most 1 minor variant (e.g., acceptable alternate transliteration) that does not cause confusion.
- Grammar: 2-3 minor errors tolerated, but sentence intent remains unambiguous.
- Reference alignment: No contradiction with Reference on key terms; minor stylistic divergence allowed.
- **Score 9**: Near-perfect, 1 trivial issue. **Score 8**: 1-2 minor issues. **Score 7**: Borderline, ~2-3 minor issues but gist intact.

**[4-6] Compromised**
- 1-2 KERR errors present (e.g., wrong digit in number, misspelled proper noun, wrong technical term).
- OR: Significant misunderstanding of a clause's meaning/syntax causing partial semantic distortion.
- OR: Omission/addition of a meaningful phrase (>10% content affected).
- Reader can still extract partial meaning but must guess or infer on distorted portions.
- Reference divergence: Key terminology contradicts Reference without justification.
- **Score 6**: Single moderate error, rest acceptable. **Score 5**: Multiple moderate errors. **Score 4**: Borderline unintelligible on 1+ clauses.

**[1-3] Serious Distortion**
- Severe hallucinations: fabricated facts, invented entities, or content with no source basis.
- >3 KERR errors, or 1+ catastrophic KERR error (e.g., completely wrong person/company name in critical context).
- Grammar logic chaotic: missing core sentence components (S/V/O), producing nonsensical output.
- Reader cannot reliably extract the source message.
- **Score 3**: Severely flawed but ~1-2 fragments salvageable. **Score 1-2**: Near-total failure. **Score 0**: Complete fabrication or empty/irrelevant output.

同传-Claude.name: Fluency
同传-Claude.description:
Contextual Coherence & Segmentation Rationality

Core Principle: Evaluate whether the translation **reads naturally in context** — as part of the Full Text discourse flow, not as an isolated sentence. Segmentation quality, cross-segment coherence, and reference resolution are judged by how well the output serves a listener/reader consuming segments sequentially.

### Band Descriptors (mutually exclusive):

**[10] Extremely Fluent**
- Segmentation boundaries align perfectly with semantic/prosodic pause points in the source.
- Referential clarity: All pronouns, demonstratives ("it", "this", "这", "其") correctly resolved — verifiable against Full Text context.
- Terminology 100% consistent with prior segments and Reference.
- Reads as natural continuous discourse when segments are concatenated.
- No awkward clause breaks, no orphaned connectives.

**[7-9] Smooth**
- Comprehensible without re-reading; information flows logically.
- 1-2 slightly rigid segmentation choices (e.g., a long clause split at a suboptimal point, but meaning preserved).
- Occasional minor term inconsistency across segments (e.g., alternating between two acceptable translations of the same term) — not causing confusion.
- Connective logic intact; reader tracks the argument without effort.
- **Score 9**: Essentially fluent, 1 minor hiccup. **Score 8**: 1-2 noticeable but non-disruptive issues. **Score 7**: Readable but with detectable "translation rhythm" in places.

**[4-6] Fragmented**
- Obvious "machine translation" feel — output reads as translated, not as natural target-language text.
- Segmentation logic errors: e.g., subject separated from verb across segments, dangling modifiers, or mid-phrase cuts.
- Referential confusion: unclear what "it/this/这" refers to without checking source.
- Requires re-reading 1+ segments to reconstruct meaning.
- Term inconsistency causes momentary confusion.
- **Score 6**: Mostly followable with effort, 1 clear segmentation/reference error. **Score 5**: Multiple flow disruptions. **Score 4**: Significant re-reading required.

**[1-3] Logic Collapse**
- Fragmented word pile-up; output is a sequence of phrases that do not form coherent semantic units.
- Word order rigidly follows source language syntax, violating target language norms.
- Segmentation produces meaningless fragments.
- Reader cannot follow the discourse thread.
- **Score 3**: Some fragments interpretable in isolation. **Score 1-2**: Near word salad. **Score 0**: Completely incoherent.

同传-Claude.name: Elegance
同传-Claude.description:
Stylistic Appropriateness & Natural Expression

Core Principle: Assess whether the translation sounds like something a competent human interpreter would say **in that specific scenario**, not like a written document being read aloud. Simultaneous interpretation should be **oral-register, clear, and natural** — avoid over-literary, over-formal, or excessively bookish phrasing. Use Full Text to determine the speaker's register (business presentation, casual discussion, technical briefing, etc.) and evaluate accordingly.

### Band Descriptors (mutually exclusive):

**[10] Native Interpreter Feel**
- Perfectly calibrated to the scenario: formal-but-spoken for business, relaxed for daily conversation, precise for technical.
- Retains speaker's pragmatic signals: hedging ("I think maybe..."), emphasis ("absolutely critical"), hesitation markers — appropriately adapted, not literally translated.
- Idiomatic target-language expressions used where appropriate (not forced).
- NOT bookish: avoids stiff written-register phrasing in spoken contexts.
- Emotional coloring preserved: urgency, humor, caution all come through.

**[7-9] Appropriate**
- Correctly distinguishes formal/informal register based on context.
- Generally natural, but slightly flat in places — e.g., a generic phrasing where an idiomatic one exists, or mildly bookish tone (偏书面但不严重) in spoken context.
- Speaker's attitude/emotion partially preserved but occasionally neutralized.
- No jarring register violations.
- **Score 9**: Nearly native, 1 slightly flat spot. **Score 8**: Competent but noticeably "translated". **Score 7**: Acceptable register, but bookish tendencies or blandness apparent in 2+ places.

**[4-6] Awkward**
- Register mismatch: e.g., using slang/colloquialisms in a formal business presentation, or archaic/literary phrasing ("hereby", "鉴于此") in casual chat.
- Emotional color largely lost: speaker sounds monotone regardless of source affect.
- Excessively bookish (过度书面化): reads like a legal document when source is a conversational meeting.
- Or excessively literal, preserving source-language idioms that don't work in target language.
- **Score 6**: Mild awkwardness, 1 clear register error. **Score 5**: Persistent register mismatch. **Score 4**: Actively distracting stylistic failures.

**[1-3] Very Poor**
- Fundamentally wrong tone: question rendered as statement, polite request as command, or vice versa.
- Potentially offensive or culturally inappropriate phrasing introduced by translation.
- Or so stylistically broken that register assessment is moot (output barely qualifies as language).
- **Score 3**: Wrong tone but not offensive. **Score 1-2**: Offensive or completely tone-deaf. **Score 0**: N/A (output not language).

- Segmentation logic errors: e.g., subject separated from verb across segments, dangling modifiers, or mid-phrase cuts.
- Referential confusion: unclear what "it/this/这" refers to without checking source.
- Requires re-reading 1+ segments to reconstruct meaning.
- Term inconsistency causes momentary confusion.
- **Score 6**: Mostly followable with effort, 1 clear segmentation/reference error. **Score 5**: Multiple flow disruptions. **Score 4**: Significant re-reading required.

**[1-3] Logic Collapse**
- Fragmented word pile-up; output is a sequence of phrases that do not form coherent semantic units.
- Word order rigidly follows source language syntax, violating target language norms.
- Segmentation produces meaningless fragments.
- Reader cannot follow the discourse thread.
- **Score 3**: Some fragments interpretable in isolation. **Score 1-2**: Near word salad. **Score 0**: Completely incoherent.

同传-Claude-032622.name: Elegance
同传-Claude-032622.description:
Stylistic Appropriateness & Natural Expression

Core Principle: Assess whether the translation sounds like something a competent human interpreter would say **in that specific scenario**, not like a written document being read aloud. Simultaneous interpretation should be **oral-register, clear, and natural** — avoid over-literary, over-formal, or excessively bookish phrasing. Use Full Text to determine the speaker's register (business presentation, casual discussion, technical briefing, etc.) and evaluate accordingly.

### Band Descriptors (mutually exclusive):

**[10] Native Interpreter Feel**
- Perfectly calibrated to the scenario: formal-but-spoken for business, relaxed for daily conversation, precise for technical.
- Retains speaker's pragmatic signals: hedging ("I think maybe..."), emphasis ("absolutely critical"), hesitation markers — appropriately adapted, not literally translated.
- Idiomatic target-language expressions used where appropriate (not forced).
- NOT bookish: avoids stiff written-register phrasing in spoken contexts.
- Emotional coloring preserved: urgency, humor, caution all come through.

**[7-9] Appropriate**
- Correctly distinguishes formal/informal register based on context.
- Generally natural, but slightly flat in places — e.g., a generic phrasing where an idiomatic one exists, or mildly bookish tone (偏书面但不严重) in spoken context.
- Speaker's attitude/emotion partially preserved but occasionally neutralized.
- No jarring register violations.
- **Score 9**: Nearly native, 1 slightly flat spot. **Score 8**: Competent but noticeably "translated". **Score 7**: Acceptable register, but bookish tendencies or blandness apparent in 2+ places.

**[4-6] Awkward**
- Register mismatch: e.g., using slang/colloquialisms in a formal business presentation, or archaic/literary phrasing ("hereby", "鉴于此") in casual chat.
- Emotional color largely lost: speaker sounds monotone regardless of source affect.
- Excessively bookish (过度书面化): reads like a legal document when source is a conversational meeting.
- Or excessively literal, preserving source-language idioms that don't work in target language.
- **Score 6**: Mild awkwardness, 1 clear register error. **Score 5**: Persistent register mismatch. **Score 4**: Actively distracting stylistic failures.

**[1-3] Very Poor**
- Fundamentally wrong tone: question rendered as statement, polite request as command, or vice versa.
- Potentially offensive or culturally inappropriate phrasing introduced by translation.
- Or so stylistically broken that register assessment is moot (output barely qualifies as language).
- **Score 3**: Wrong tone but not offensive. **Score 1-2**: Offensive or completely tone-deaf. **Score 0**: N/A (output not language).

同传-Claude-032622.name: KERR_Errors
同传-Claude-032622.description: Count of errors in Key Entities (Names, Places, Numbers, Currencies, Terms).

同传-Claude-032622.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传-Claude-032622.description: Is the segmentation reasonable? Check for cut-off sentences or incomplete logic.

同传-Claude-032622.name: ToneMatch
同传-Claude-032622.description: Does the translation style match the source context and emotion?

同传-Claude-032622.name: Hallucination_Strategy
同传-Claude-032622.description: How did the model handle Source/ASR errors?

同传-GPT.name: Accuracy
同传-GPT.description:
Core: information preservation + KERR (key entities). Judge against Global Context + Segment Context.

What to check (must consider):
- Information fidelity: who/does what/to whom; conditions; causality; polarity; time order.
- KERR: names, places, orgs, numbers, dates, currency, technical terms, acronyms, proper nouns.
- Hallucination: adding events/relations/attributes not supported by Source+Context.
- Grammar impact: grammar errors that change meaning or cause ambiguity reduce Accuracy.

Special tolerance (Filler-only):
If the source segment is mostly stance/filler particles (e.g., repeated “好/嗯/对/行/啊”) with little propositional content,
reasonable simplification/condensation is allowed, as long as the pragmatic function (acknowledgement/confirmation/transition) is preserved.

ASR-correction rule (bounded):
If Segment INPUT contains an obvious ASR error but Segment ANSWER corrects it using Global Context evidence, do NOT penalize;
you MAY award up to +1 Accuracy (cap) compared to what a literal-ASR-following translation would get.
If Segment ANSWER blindly follows the ASR error while context clearly indicates the correct meaning, penalize moderately.

Accuracy Band Guidance:
- [10] Precise: perfectly preserves meaning; KERR 100% correct; zero hallucination.
       Grammar: perfect; allow only 0–1 tiny preposition/article differences that do not create ambiguity.
- [7–9] Mostly accurate: main meaning correct, but minor non-key details missing (e.g., some filler/stance words),
       OR <10% minor additions/omissions that do not affect core factual judgment.
       Some noticeable grammar errors allowed if they do not block understanding of main meaning.
- [4–6] Damaged: 1–2 KERR errors (e.g., wrong digit count), OR clear lexical misunderstanding / major grammar/syntax errors
       that distort part of the information.
- [1-3] Severe distortion: serious hallucination (unsupported invention), OR >3 critical errors,
       OR translation is unrelated to source.
       Grammar/logic chaotic (missing core SVO/relations), essentially not understandable.

Hard floor rules (override bands if triggered):
- Meaning reversal (affirmative↔negative, must↔must-not, before↔after, cause↔effect) => Accuracy must be 1-3.
- Fabricated named entity or fabricated numeric claim that changes facts => usually 1-3 (unless clearly a benign typo with no factual effect).

同传-GPT.name: Fluency
同传-GPT.description:
Core: contextual coherence + segmentation rationality + readability in the target language.

What to check:
- Segmentation: clause boundaries match meaning pauses; no “subject-predicate split” across segments causing break.
- Coherence: can be read in sequence with Segment Context; pronoun/reference is clear (“it/this/that” points to correct antecedent).
- Terminology consistency: same term rendered consistently across nearby segments unless context demands change.
- Target-language naturalness: word order, collocations, idiomaticity (note: style belongs mainly to Elegance unless it harms readability).

Streaming tolerance (your rule):
If clauses are basically complete and the translation is “broken but connectable” across adjacent context, it may still be 7–10.
But if the first half/clause lacks core information and forces the reader to guess, deduct accordingly.

Fluency Band Guidance:
- [10] Extremely fluent: segmentation perfectly matches semantic pauses; references are clear; terminology consistent throughout.
- [7–9] Smooth: understandable with little friction; 1–2 slightly awkward segmentation points (e.g., long sentence chopped),
       OR occasional inconsistency in term rendering that does not harm understanding.
- [4–6] Fragmented: obvious MT feel; segmentation logic errors (e.g., subject-verb separation), unclear references;
       requires rereading to infer intended meaning.
- [1–3] Collapsed: word salad / rigid wrong word order; cannot form coherent semantic logic.

同传-GPT.name: Elegance
同传-GPT.description:
Core: register/style/tone appropriateness + idiomatic, native-like expression (WITHOUT masking accuracy errors).

What to check:
- Register match: formal vs informal; business vs casual; appropriate politeness level.
- Tone/emotion preservation: hesitation, tact, firmness, humor/sarcasm where relevant.
- Idiomaticity: natural phrasing, collocations, rhythm; avoids awkward calques.
- Cultural adaptation: can handle culture-specific expressions (slang, idioms, quotes/poetry) appropriately.

Non-compensation rule:
Do not raise Accuracy because Elegance is good. Elegance can only reflect style/tone/naturalness.
If tone is fundamentally wrong (question→statement; polite→rude), Elegance must drop sharply even if grammar is correct.

Elegance Band Guidance (added):
- [10] Native-level: perfectly fits the scenario (e.g., business meeting vs casual chat), preserves emotion/stance
       (hesitation, tact, determination), refined word choice, and appropriately adapts culture-specific expressions.
- [7–9] Appropriate: register is mostly correct and polite/neutral as needed, but phrasing may be plain, slightly bookish,
       or lacking “native flavor.”
- [4–6] Awkward/mismatched: register clashes with context (e.g., slang in business, archaic/obscure phrasing in daily talk),
       emotional color largely lost.
- [1-3] Very poor: tone is completely wrong (e.g., turns questions into statements), or uses offensive/inappropriate language.

同传-GPT-0331.name: Fluency
同传-GPT-0331.description:
Core: contextual coherence + segmentation rationality + readability in the target language.

What to check:
- Segmentation: clause boundaries match meaning pauses; no “subject-predicate split” across segments causing break.
- Coherence: can be read in sequence with Segment Context; pronoun/reference is clear (“it/this/that” points to correct antecedent).

- Target-language naturalness: word order, collocations, idiomaticity (note: style belongs mainly to Elegance unless it harms readability).

Streaming tolerance (your rule):
If clauses are basically complete and the translation is “broken but connectable” across adjacent context, it may still be 7–10.
But if the first half/clause lacks any core information and forces the reader to guess, deduct accordingly.
Respect structural differences between source and target languages. When meaning is fully preserved—assessed contextually across adjacent segments—the translation may be restructured freely, with no penalty.

Fluency Band Guidance:
- [10] Extremely fluent: segmentation perfectly matches semantic pauses; references are clear; terminology consistent throughout.
- [7–9] Smooth: understandable with little friction; 1–2 slightly awkward segmentation points (e.g., long sentence chopped),
       OR occasional inconsistency in term rendering that does not harm understanding.
- [4–6] Fragmented: obvious MT feel; segmentation logic errors (e.g., subject-verb separation), unclear references;
       requires rereading to infer intended meaning.
- [1–3] Collapsed: word salad / rigid wrong word order; cannot form coherent semantic logic.

What to check:

- Idiomaticity: natural phrasing, collocations, rhythm; avoids awkward calques.
- Cultural adaptation: can handle culture-specific expressions (slang, idioms, quotes/poetry) appropriately.

Elegance Band Guidance (added):
- [10] Native-level: perfectly fits the scenario (e.g., business meeting vs casual chat), preserves emotion/stance
       (hesitation, tact, determination), refined word choice, and appropriately adapts culture-specific expressions.
- [7–9] Appropriate: register is mostly correct and polite/neutral as needed, but phrasing may be plain, slightly bookish,
       or lacking “native flavor.”
- [4–6] Awkward/mismatched: register clashes with context (e.g., slang in business, archaic/obscure phrasing in daily talk),
       emotional color largely lost.
- [1-3] Very poor: tone is completely wrong (e.g., turns questions into statements), or uses offensive/inappropriate language.

同传-GPT-0402.name: Accuracy
同传-GPT-0402.description:
**Core**: preservation of meaning, proposition, logic, and key entities.

Judge against:
- Segment Reference Text (if provided)
- Global Context Reference
- Segment Context
- raw ASR only as lower-priority evidence

Check:
- who / does what / to whom
- polarity
- time order
- conditions
- causality
- stance and intent
- KERR: names, places, orgs, numbers, dates, currencies, titles, technical terms
- hallucination / unsupported additions
- grammar/syntax errors that change meaning

#### Special tolerance: filler-only segments
If the source segment is mostly fillers / acknowledgement / stance particles with little propositional content,
reasonable condensation is allowed **only if pragmatic function is preserved**.

#### ASR-correction rule (bounded)
If the answer correctly repairs an obvious ASR error using strong context/reference evidence:
- do **not** penalize
- you may award up to **+1 Accuracy** compared to a literal ASR-following version
- but total Accuracy still cannot exceed what the semantic quality justifies

If the answer follows a context-obviously wrong ASR reading, penalize moderately or heavily depending on semantic damage.

#### Accuracy Band Guidance
- **[10] Precise**: meaning fully preserved; KERR fully correct; no hallucination; no material loss
- **[7–9] Mostly accurate**: main meaning correct; only minor non-key omissions/additions; no core factual distortion
- **[4–6] Damaged**: 1–2 meaningful lexical/KERR errors, or partial distortion, or clear key omission
- **[1–3] Severe distortion**: logic reversal, serious hallucination, unrelated translation, or multiple critical failures

#### Hard floor rules
- Polarity / logic reversal => Accuracy **1–3**
- Fabricated named entity or fabricated number changing facts => usually **1–3**
- Any single critical number/date/entity/title error that changes business meaning => Accuracy **≤4**
- ≥2 critical KERR errors => Accuracy **≤4**
- >10% meaningful omission => Accuracy **≤6**
- Empty / near-empty answer with substantive source => Accuracy **≤2**

同传-GPT-0402.name: Fluency
同传-GPT-0402.description:
**Core**: readability, local coherence, contextual connectability, segmentation rationality.

Check:
- segmentation respects semantic structure
- local completeness of clause/proposition
- recoverability using adjacent context
- reference clarity
- terminology consistency across nearby segments
- natural target-language syntax

#### Streaming tolerance
If the translation is somewhat broken locally **but clearly connectable** across adjacent segments and preserves the intended relation, Fluency may still fall in **7–9**.

But deduct when:
- a clause lacks core information and forces guessing
- subject/predicate are split destructively
- conditions/comparisons/negation are broken apart
- reference becomes unclear due to segmentation
- local output reads as fragment rather than usable streaming translation

#### Fluency Band Guidance
- **[10] Extremely fluent**: natural, coherent, segmentation aligns with meaning pauses
- **[7–9] Smooth**: understandable with minor awkwardness; some streaming fragmentation but recoverable
- **[4–6] Fragmented**: obvious breakage, awkward parse, rereading needed, segmentation damages readability
- **[1–3] Collapsed**: incoherent, unusable, cannot form stable semantic reading

#### Hard rules
- Material segmentation damage => Fluency **≤6**
- Unrecoverable segmentation break / severe fragment => Fluency **≤3**
- Empty / near-empty answer with substantive source => Fluency **≤3**

同传-GPT-0402.name: Elegance
同传-GPT-0402.description:
**Core**: appropriateness of register, tone, idiomaticity, and native-likeness.

Check:
- formal vs informal match
- politeness level
- emotional color / stance
- idiomatic phrasing
- avoidance of calque / bookish mismatch
- culture-specific adaptation when relevant

#### Non-compensation rule
Elegance reflects style only. It cannot rescue semantic errors.

#### Elegance Band Guidance
- **[10] Native-level**: precise tone, appropriate register, idiomatic and context-fit
- **[7–9] Appropriate**: mostly natural and suitable, though plain / slightly stiff / slightly bookish
- **[4–6] Awkward or mismatched**: style clashes, emotional color lost, unnatural phrasing
- **[1–3] Very poor**: tone fundamentally wrong, offensively inappropriate, or pragmatically broken

#### Hard rules
- Severe tone/register mismatch => Elegance **≤3**
- Moderate mismatch / obvious bookishness in casual speech => Elegance **≤6**

同传-GPT-0402.name: KERR_Errors
同传-GPT-0402.description:
Count key-entity errors only:
- Names
- Places
- Organizations
- Numbers
- Dates
- Currency
- Titles / roles
- Technical terms / product terms / acronyms

Use `Severe_Hallucination` only if the output invents a key entity / numeric claim / factual element unsupported by source and context.

同传-GPT-0402.name: Segmentation_Issues
同传-GPT-0402.description:
- `true` if segmentation/cutoff materially harms parsing, local completeness, or reference resolution
- `false` otherwise

Important:
- Mere split/merge is **not** an issue if meaning remains recoverable and coherent.

同传-GPT-0402.name: ToneMatch
同传-GPT-0402.description:
- `true` if source tone / stance / politeness is basically preserved
- `false` if materially shifted

同传-GPT-0402.name: Hallucination_Strategy
同传-GPT-0402.description:
Decision rule:
- `Corrected_ASR_Error`: answer improves on a wrong ASR reading using context/reference
- `Followed_ASR_Error`: answer reproduces an ASR mistake contradicted by stronger evidence
- `Introduced_New_Hallucination`: unsupported invention not licensed by source/context
- `Neutral`: none of the above
